rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE KONSTIN FAMILY REVOCABLE TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent Trustee J. Konstin'S Noitce Of Motion For Judgement On Pleadings.
rulings.Rulings: Assuming that the trial court retains jurisdiction to decide this motion given the pending appeal from the Court's order sustaining Respondent's demurrer on other causes of action (an issue the parties have not raised or briefed), the Court denies the motion both because (1) it is procedurally improper as there has been no change in law since the Court overruled the demurrer on the remaining cause of action, which was also based on the petition failing to state a claim on which relief could be granted (CCP section 438(g)), and (2) the petition sufficiently states a cause of action by alleging a breach of fiduciary duty and resulting damages at least as to appropriating Dorchester rents and failing to keep petitioners apprised. Respondent's arguments may go to the likelihood of success on the merits of petitioners' claims, but they do not establish that those claims may not proceed at this stage. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing in court must so advise opposing parties and notify the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) by 4:30 p.m. on Wednesday, December 12, 2012.
